explanation: The property states that globally (G), if apnea is detected (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) an apnea alarm shall be triggered (bool_exp3). This perfectly captures the causal relationship stated in the natural language: 'If apnea is detected, an apnea alarm shall be triggered.'

The property states that globally, if 'apnea' (apnea is detected) is true, then 'apneaAlarm' (an apnea alarm shall be triggered) must also be true. This directly captures the conditional requirement specified in the natural language.

The property states that globally (G), if apnea is detected (apnea), then an apnea alarm shall be triggered (apneaAlarm). This directly translates the conditional statement 'If apnea is detected, an apnea alarm shall be triggered' into LTL, ensuring that the alarm is triggered whenever apnea is detected.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected (apnea), then an apnea alarm shall be triggered (apneaAlarm). This is a direct translation of 'If A, then B shall be triggered' into the LTL format G(A -> B).

The property states that globally (G), if apnea is detected (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) an apnea alarm shall be triggered (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that an apnea detection must always be followed by an apnea alarm at some point in the future.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected (bool_exp1), then an apnea alarm must be triggered (bool_exp3). This directly translates the conditional statement 'If apnea is detected, an apnea alarm shall be triggered' into a global implication.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected, then an apnea alarm shall be triggered. This directly translates the conditional cause-effect relationship in the natural language into an LTL implication that must always hold.

The property states that globally, if apnea is detected, then an apnea alarm shall be triggered. This directly translates the 'If A, then B' causal relationship into a global implication.
